databricksrepo.notes: databricksrepo remains completely empty (README-only) for a second consecutive spotlight cycle; both prior fixes (real README, stub notebook) were ignored, now escalated. New findings shift focus to governance gaps: no license, no .gitignore, no CI/CD or Databricks Asset Bundle scaffolding, and a non-descriptive repo name. Recommended first fix: write a real purpose-driven README plus a databricks.yml bundle skeleton to convert this from a dead placeholder into an actual project shell.

markets-brief.notes: markets-brief is a clean, deterministic Telegram agent (Yahoo Finance quotes, no LLM) with solid offline unit tests and a well-reasoned dual-cron dedupe guard in CI. Main findings: quote()'s dual-host fallback swallows which host/symbol failed (diagnosability gap), send_telegram's chunk-retry has an undocumented partial-send edge case, and main()'s per-symbol failure isolation lacks a dedicated test. No prior notes to follow up on — this is the first review of this repo.

news-briefing.notes: news-briefing: solid two-stage LLM pipeline with strong defensive design (link validation, dual seen/briefed memory, non-fatal enrichments). Found test_links.py truncated/broken as shown, a token-based link validator that may miss markdown-style [text](url) injection, and redundant duplicate fetches for og:image + article text on the same URL. No prior notes to follow up on.
